name: Feedly Pro
price_basis: Pro at $6 per month billed annually, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KILL: $72 a year to read free XML. Zo polls your feeds, dedupes the overlap, and hands you a morning brief.
what_zo_builds: An RSS reader shaped like a briefing: Zo polls your feeds on a schedule, dedupes stories that show up in five places at once, filters noise by rules you write in plain language, and delivers a morning digest with links back to the sources. A thousand feeds is a config file, not a paid tier.
dont_kill_if: Your team runs shared boards and newsletters out of Feedly, or the AI filtering across thousands of sources is part of your job. The solo reading case is what dies here.
build_brief: Goal: replace Feedly Pro with a feed pipeline I own. Export my OPML from Feedly. Set up a scheduled pull that fetches every feed, dedupes stories that appear in multiple sources, and applies my mute rules. Render a morning digest note with the day's items grouped by topic, plus a weekly roundup of what got filtered so I can tune the rules. Run alongside Feedly for a week to confirm nothing important slips through. Done when the digest arrives daily, the mute rules hold, and the plan is canceled.
